readme.text:
# Hulunote

**AI + Note Hippocampus** — Your second brain, powered by AI.

Hulunote is an open-source outliner note-taking application with bidirectional linking. Inspired by Roam Research, designed for networked thought. Like the hippocampus organizes memories in the brain, Hulunote organizes your knowledge — and now AI helps you build, connect, and retrieve it.

![](./images/demo1.png)
![](./images/graph-sidebar.png)
![](./images/ios-demo-all.png)

## Features

- **Outliner Structure** — Organize thoughts in hierarchical bullet points with infinite nesting
- **Bidirectional Links** — Connect ideas with `[[wiki-style links]]` and backlinks
- **Daily Notes** — Journaling with automatic date-based pages
- **Multiple Databases** — Separate workspaces for different projects
- **Built-in MCP Client** — Connect to AI models and MCP servers directly from the app
- **AI Note Generation** — Let AI organize, summarize, and generate notes for you

## AI Integration

Hulunote embraces the **AI + Note Hippocampus** philosophy: AI acts as your cognitive co-pilot, helping you capture, organize, and retrieve knowledge just like the hippocampus does for memory.

### MCP Server — For Claude Desktop & MCP Clients

[hulunote-mcp-server](https://github.com/hulunote/hulunote-mcp-server) lets any MCP-compatible client (Claude Desktop, etc.) read and write your Hulunote notes via natural language:

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
python hulunote_mcp.py
```

```
"List my databases" → "Create a note about quantum computing" → "Add outline nodes with key concepts"
```

### OpenClaw Plugin — For AI Agents

[openclaw-hulunote-assistant](https://github.com/hulunote/openclaw-hulunote-assistant) brings Hulunote into the OpenClaw AI agent platform, enabling autonomous note management:

```bash
openclaw plugins install --link /path/to/openclaw-hulunote-assistant
```

AI agents can browse databases, create notes, manage outlines, search across your knowledge base, and auto-organize information — all through natural conversation.

### What AI + Hulunote Can Do

- **Auto-organize research** — AI reads sources and creates structured notes with bidirectional links
- **Knowledge summarization** — Condense long documents into concise outline nodes
- **Smart Q&A** — Ask questions about your notes, get answers with references
- **Note generation** — Describe a topic, AI creates a full note with hierarchical outline
- **Cross-database search** — AI finds and connects related ideas across all your databases

## Quick Start

```bash
# 1. Initialize database
createdb hulunote_open
psql -d hulunote_open -f hulunote-rust/init.sql

# 2. Start backend
cd hulunote-rust && cargo run

# 3. Start frontend dev server
cd hulunote && yarn && npx shadow-cljs watch hulunote

# 4. Open browser at http://localhost:6689
```

**Test Account (for local/dev use):** `chanshunli@gmail.com` / `123456`

## Configuration

- **Backend API Base URL (dev):** configured via `:closure-defines` in `shadow-cljs.edn` (`hulunote.http/API_BASE_URL`).

## Screenshots

TUI:
![](./images/demo-tui.png)

MCP client:
![](./images/demo1-mcp-chat.png)
![](./images/demo1-mcp-setting.png)

## Repositories

| Repository | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| [hulunote](https://github.com/hulunote/hulunote) | Frontend (this repo) |
| [hulunote-rust](https://github.com/hulunote/hulunote-rust) | Backend (Rust) |
| [hulunote-mcp-server](https://github.com/hulunote/hulunote-mcp-server) | MCP Server for Claude Desktop & MCP clients |
| [openclaw-hulunote-assistant](https://github.com/hulunote/openclaw-hulunote-assistant) | OpenClaw AI agent plugin |
| [hulunote-tui](https://github.com/hulunote/hulunote-tui) | Terminal UI |
| [hulunote-app](https://github.com/hulunote/hulunote-app) | Desktop App (Electron) |
| [hulunote-android](https://github.com/hulunote/hulunote-android) | Android |
| [hulunote-ios](https://github.com/hulunote/hulunote-ios) | iOS |

## Contributing

See `CONTRIBUTING.md`.

## License

This project is licensed under the MIT License - see the [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details.

items.name: 🚀 Hulunote v1.1.0 — MCP Reasoning & Major UI Upgrade
items.body_excerpt:
# 🚀 Hulunote v1.1.0 — MCP Reasoning & Major UI Upgrade

This release focuses on **AI-powered note operations and a major UI upgrade**.
Hulunote now supports **MCP reflective reasoning**, enabling AI to think through note structures and perform batch operations across your knowledge base.

---

## ✨ Highlights

### 🧠 MCP Reflective Reasoning

Hulunote now supports **reflection-based reasoning through MCP**.

AI can:

* Analyze note structures
* Perform **multi-step reasoning**
* Execute **batch note organization tasks**

This allows AI agents to **think before acting**, improving accuracy when working with large knowledge bases.

---

### 🔌 Hulunote MCP Server Integration

You can now connect directly to the **Hulunote MCP Server**.

This enables:

* AI-driven **batch note organization**
* Automated knowledge restructuring
* Programmatic note operations through MCP tools

Your notes are no longer just documents —
they become **structured data that AI can operate on**.

---

### 🖱 Major UI Upgrade

The editor interface has been significantly improved.

New capabilities include:

* **Drag-and-drop node movement**
* More fluid outliner interactions
* Improved layout and usability

Working with large outlines is now much faster.

---

### 💡 Syntax Highlighting

Code blocks now support **syntax highlighting**, making technical notes and code snippets easier to read.

---

## 🏗 Stack

Rust (Axum, SQLx) + ClojureScript + PostgreSQL

---

## 🎯 Vision

Hulunote continues to push toward a new workflow:

> **Notes are not static documents — they are structured knowledge that AI can reason about and operate on.**

With **MCP reasoning + tool integration**, your knowledge base becomes an **AI-operable workspace**.

---

Feedback and contributions welcome.

items.name: 🚀 Hulunote v0.1.0 — First Public Release
items.body_excerpt:
# 🚀 Hulunote v0.1.0 — First Public Release

Hulunote is now publicly available.

An open-source outliner note app **with a built-in MCP client** — turning your knowledge base into an AI tool workspace.

---

## ✨ Core Features

### 🧩 Built-in MCP Client (Major Feature)

Hulunote works like **Claude Desktop / Cherry Studio / VS Code AI tool integrations**.

You can:

* Connect to **your own MCP servers**
* Configure custom tools
* Use AI tools directly inside your notes

Your note app becomes a **local AI tool hub**.

---

### 📝 Outliner Notes

* Infinite nested bullet structure
* Fast structured thinking workflow

---

### 🔗 Bidirectional Links

* `[[wiki-style links]]`
* Automatic backlinks
* Networked knowledge instead of folders

---

### 📆 Daily Notes

Automatic date-based journaling.

### 🗂 Multiple Databases

Separate workspaces for different projects.

---

## 🏗 Stack

Rust (Axum, SQLx) + ClojureScript + PostgreSQL

---

## 🎯 What This Release Proves

Hulunote shows that:

> **Notes + Knowledge Graph + MCP Tools can live in one workspace.**

Not just writing.
Not just AI chat.
But a structured thinking environment that can call tools.

---

Feedback and contributions welcome.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/hulunote/hulunote/commits/v0.1.0

Getting Started • Basic Function • Documentation

## Our online channels [![Discord](https://img.shields.io/discord/844351092758413353?color=blueviolet&label=Discord&logo=discord&style=flat)](https://discord.gg/JU5JGGKGNG) [![Telegram Group](https://img.shields.io/badge/Telegram-Group-32AFED?logo=telegram)](https://t.me/wppconnect) [![WhatsApp Group](https://img.shields.io/badge/WhatsApp-Group-25D366?logo=whatsapp)](https://chat.whatsapp.com/LJaQu6ZyNvnBPNAVRbX00K) [![YouTube](https://img.shields.io/youtube/channel/subscribers/UCD7J9LG08PmGQrF5IS7Yv9A?label=YouTube)](https://www.youtube.com/c/wppconnect) ## Functions | | | | ---------------------------------------------------------- | --- | | Automatic QR Refresh | ✔ | | Send **text, image, video, audio and docs** | ✔ | | Get **contacts, chats, groups, group members, Block List** | ✔ | | Send contacts | ✔ | | Send stickers | ✔ | | Send stickers GIF | ✔ | | Multiple Sessions | ✔ | | Forward Messages | ✔ | | Receive message | ✔ | | insert user section | ✔ | | Send _location_ | ✔ | | **and much more** | ✔ | See more at WhatsApp methods ## Installation The first thing that you had to do is install the `npm package` : ```bash npm i --save @wppconnect-team/wppconnect ``` See more at Getting Started ## Development Building WPPConnect is really simple, to build the entire project just run ```bash > npm run build ``` # Update checking Whatsapp is in constant change. In order to tackle this issue, we suggest always keeping your Wppconnect package up-to-date. The method/function names won't change, only their core algorithm. This way you won't have to makes changes in your code at every update. They will remain the same forever but might experience deprecation. ## Maintainers Maintainers are needed, I cannot keep with all the updates by myself. If you are interested please open a Pull Request. ## Contributing Pull requests are welcome. For major changes, please open an issue first to discuss what you would like to change. ## Star History [![Star History Chart](https://api.star-history.com/svg?repos=wppconnect-team/wppconnect,wppconnect-team/wa-js&type=Date)](https://star-history.com/#wppconnect-team/wppconnect&wppconnect-team/wa-js&Date) ## License This file is part of WPPConnect. repo_cards.description: Open-source agentic data engineering harness for dbt, SQL, and cloud warehouses. 100+ tools, 10 warehouses, AI-powered.

readme.text:
**The open-source data engineering harness.** The intelligence layer for data engineering AI — 100+ deterministic tools for SQL analysis, column-level lineage, dbt, FinOps, and warehouse connectivity across every major cloud platform. Run standalone in your terminal, embed underneath Claude Code or Codex, or integrate into CI pipelines and orchestration DAGs. Precision data tooling for any LLM. [![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/altimate-code)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/altimate-code) [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](./LICENSE) [![Slack](https://img.shields.io/badge/Slack-Join%20Community-4A154B?logo=slack)](https://altimate.studio/join-agentic-data-engineering-slack) [![Docs](https://img.shields.io/badge/docs-docs.altimate.sh-blue)](https://docs.altimate.sh)
--- ## Install ```bash npm install -g altimate-code ``` Then — in order: **Step 1: Configure your LLM provider** (required before anything works): ```bash altimate # Launch the TUI /connect # Interactive setup — choose your provider and enter your API key ``` Or set an environment variable directly: ```bash export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your_key # Anthropic Claude export OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key # OpenAI ``` **Step 2 (optional): Auto-detect your data stack** (read-only, safe for production connections): ```bash altimate /discover ``` `/discover` auto-detects dbt projects, warehouse connections (from `~/.dbt/profiles.yml`, Docker, environment variables), and installed tools (dbt, sqlfluff, airflow, dagster, and more). Skip this and start building — you can always run it later. > **Headless / scripted usage:** `altimate --yolo` auto-approves all permission prompts. Not recommended with live warehouse connections. > **Zero additional setup.** One command install. ## Why a specialized harness? General AI coding agents can edit SQL files. They cannot *understand* your data stack. altimate gives any LLM a deterministic data engineering intelligence layer — no hallucinated SQL advice, no guessing at schema, no missed PII. | Capability | General coding agents | altimate | |---|---|---| | SQL anti-pattern detection | None | 19 rules, confidence-scored | | Column-level lineage | None | Automatic from SQL, any dialect | | Schema-aware autocomplete | None | Live-indexed warehouse metadata | | Cross-dialect SQL translation | None | Snowflake ↔ BigQuery ↔ Databricks ↔ Redshift | | FinOps & cost analysis | None | Credits, expensive queries, right-sizing | | PII detection | None | 30+ regex patterns, 15 categories | | dbt integration | Basic file editing | Manifest parsing, test gen, model scaffolding, lineage | | Data visualization | None | Auto-generated charts from SQL results | | Observability | None | Local-first tracing of AI sessions and tool calls | > **Benchmarked precision:** 100% F1 on SQL anti-pattern detection (1,077 queries, 19 rules, 0 false positives). > 100% edge-match on column-level lineage (500 queries, 13 categories). > [See methodology →](experiments/BENCHMARKS.md) **What the harness provides:** - **SQL Intelligence Engine** — deterministic SQL parsing and analysis (not LLM pattern matching). 19 rules, 100% F1, 0 false positives. Built for data engineers who've been burned by hallucinated SQL advice. - **Column-Level Lineage** — automatic extraction from SQL across dialects. 100% edge-match on 500 benchmark queries. - **Live Warehouse Intelligence** — indexed schemas, query history, and cost data from your actual warehouse. Not guesses. - **dbt Native** — manifest parsing, test generation, model scaffolding, medallion patterns, impact analysis - **FinOps** — credit consumption, expensive query detection, warehouse right-sizing, idle resource cleanup - **PII Detection** — 15 categories, 30+ regex patterns, enforced pre-execution **Works seamlessly with Claude Code and Codex.** Use `/configure-claude` or `/configure-codex` to set up integration in one step. altimate is the data engineering tool layer — use it standalone in your terminal, or mount it as the harness underneath whatever AI agent you already run. The two are complementary. altimate-code is a fork of [OpenCode](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode) rebuilt for data teams. Model-agnostic — bring your own LLM or run locally with Ollama. ## Quick demo ```bash # Auto-detect your data stack (dbt projects, warehouse connections, installed tools) > /discover # Analyze a query for anti-patterns and optimization opportunities > Analyze this query for issues: SELECT * FROM orders JOIN customers ON orders.id = customers.order_id # Translate SQL across dialects > /sql-translate this Snowflake query to BigQuery: SELECT DATEADD(day, 7, current_date()) # Generate dbt tests for a model > /generate-tests for models/staging/stg_orders.sql # Get a cost report for your Snowflake account > /cost-report ``` ## Key Features All features are deterministic — they parse, trace, and measure. Not LLM pattern matching. ### SQL Anti-Pattern Detection 19 rules with confidence scoring — catches SELECT *, cartesian joins, non-sargable predicates, correlated subqueries, and more. **100% accuracy** on 1,077 benchmark queries. ### Column-Level Lineage Automatic lineage extraction from SQL. Trace any column back through joins, CTEs, and subqueries to its source. Works standalone or with dbt manifests for project-wide lineage. **100% edge match** on 500 benchmark queries. ### FinOps & Cost Analysis Credit analysis, expensive query detection, warehouse right-sizing, unused resource cleanup, and RBAC auditing. ### Cross-Dialect Translation Transpile SQL between Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks, Redshift, PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQL Server, and DuckDB. ### PII Detection & Safety Automatic column scanning for PII across 15 categories with 30+ regex patterns. Safety checks and policy enforcement before query execution. ### dbt Native Manifest parsing, test generation, model scaffolding, incremental model detection, and lineage-aware refactoring. 12 purpose-built skills including medallion patterns, yaml config generation, and dbt docs. ### Data Visualization Interactive charts and dashboards from SQL results. The data-viz skill generates publication-ready visualizations with automatic chart type selection based on your data. ### Local-First Tracing Built-in observability for AI interactions — trace tool calls, token usage, and session activity locally. No external services required. View traces with `altimate trace`. ### AI Teammate Training Teach your AI teammate project-specific patterns, naming conventions, and best practices. The training system learns from examples and applies rules automatically across sessions. ## Agent Modes Each mode has scoped permissions, tool access, and SQL write-access control. | Mode | Role | Access | |---|---|---| | **Builder** | Create dbt models, SQL pipelines, and data transformations | Full read/write (write SQL prompts for approval; `DROP DATABASE`/`DROP SCHEMA`/`TRUNCATE` hard-blocked) | | **Analyst** | Explore data, run SELECT queries, FinOps analysis, and generate insights | Read-only enforced (SELECT only, no file writes) | | **Plan** | Outline an approach before acting | Minimal (read files only, no SQL or bash) | > **New to altimate?** Start with **Analyst mode** — it's read-only and safe to run against production connections. Need specialized workflows (validation, migration, research)? Create [custom agent modes](https://docs.altimate.sh). ## Supported Warehouses Snowflake · BigQuery · Databricks · PostgreSQL · Redshift · DuckDB · MySQL · SQL Server · Oracle · SQLite First-class support with schema indexing, query execution, and metadata introspection. SSH tunneling available for secure connections. ## Works with Any LLM Model-agnostic — bring your own provider or run locally. Anthropic · OpenAI · Google Gemini · Google Vertex AI · Amazon Bedrock · Azure OpenAI · Mistral · Groq · DeepInfra · Cerebras · Cohere · Together AI · Perplexity · xAI · OpenRouter · Ollama · GitHub Copilot ## Skills altimate ships with built-in skills for every common data engineering task — type `/` in the TUI to browse available skills and get autocomplete. No memorization required. ## Community & Contributing - **Slack**: [Join Slack](https://altimate.studio/join-agentic-data-engineering-slack) — Real-time chat for questions, showcases, and feature discussion - **Issues**: [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/AltimateAI/altimate-code/issues) — Bug reports and feature requests - **Discussions**: [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/AltimateAI/altimate-code/discussions) — Long-form questions and proposals - **Security**: See [SECURITY.md](./SECURITY.md) for responsible disclosure Contributions welcome — docs, SQL rules, warehouse connectors, and TUI improvements are all needed. The contributing guide covers setup, the vouch system, and the issue-first PR policy. **[Read CONTRIBUTING.md →](./CONTRIBUTING.md)** ## Changelog - **v0.5.0** (March 2026) — smooth streaming mode, builtin skills via postinstall, `/configure-claude` and `/configure-codex` commands, warehouse auth hardening - **v0.4.9** (March 2026) — Snowflake auth overhaul (all auth methods), dbt tool regression fixes, parallel CI builds - **v0.4.2** (March 2026) — yolo mode, Python engine elimination (all-native TypeScript), tool consolidation, path sandboxing hardening, altimate-dbt CLI, unscoped npm package - **v0.4.0** (March 2026) — data visualization skill, 100+ tools, training system - **v0.3.x** — [See full changelog →](CHANGELOG.md) ## License MIT — see [LICENSE](./LICENSE). ## Acknowledgements altimate is a fork of [OpenCode](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode), the open-source AI coding agent. We build on top of their excellent foundation to add data-team-specific capabilities.

readme.text:
# SpectaQL [![npm][npm]][npm-url] [![downloads][npm-downloads]][npm-url] SpectaQL is a Node.js library that generates static documentation for a [GraphQL](https://graphql.org) schema using a variety of options: 1. From a live endpoint using the introspection query. 2. From a file containing an introspection query result. 3. From a file, files or glob leading to the schema definitions in SDL. The goal of SpectaQL is to help you keep your documentation complete, current and beautiful with the least amount of pain as possible. --- **Repository sponsored by [Anvil](www.useanvil.com/developers)** ![Horizontal Lockupblack](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/293079/169453889-ae211c6c-7634-4ccd-8ca9-8970c2621b6f.png#gh-light-mode-only) ![Horizontal Lockup copywhite](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/293079/169453892-895f637b-4633-4a14-b997-960c9e17579b.png#gh-dark-mode-only) Anvil provides easy APIs for all things paperwork. 1. [PDF filling API](https://www.useanvil.com/products/pdf-filling-api/) - fill out a PDF template with a web request and structured JSON data. 2. [PDF generation API](https://www.useanvil.com/products/pdf-generation-api/) - send markdown or HTML and Anvil will render it to a PDF. 3. [Etch e-sign with API](https://www.useanvil.com/products/etch/) - customizable, embeddable, e-signature platform with an API to control the signing process end-to-end. 4. [Anvil Workflows (w/ API)](https://www.useanvil.com/products/workflows/) - Webforms + PDF + e-sign with a powerful no-code builder. Easily collect structured data, generate PDFs, and request signatures. Learn more on our [Anvil developer page](https://www.useanvil.com/developers/). --- ### NOTICE Migrating from pre `1.0`? - Here are the breaking changes: [BREAKING_CHANGES_1.md][breaking-1] - Here's a list of some of the interesting new changes: [CHANGELOG.md][changelog] --- Out of the box, SpectaQL delivers a 3-column page with a modern look and feel. However, many aspects can be customized with ease, and just about everything can be customized if you're willing to dig in. SpectaQL also has a lot of advanced features and ways to enhance your GraphQL documentation. :tada:  [Anvil](https://www.useanvil.com) uses SpectaQL for our own docs, and you can see them [here][docs]. :tada:
:tada:  This supporting [blog post][blog] outlines our use-case and implementation, and may be worth a read :tada: ## Benefits Using SpectaQL to generate your documentation has a number of benefits, such as: - Your documentation will always be up-to-date - Your documentation will always be complete - Your documentation can be beautiful and "on brand" - Save developers time and headache by not having to write documentation ## Features - Various ways to ingest your schema: - Hit a live GraphQL endpoint with an introspection query - Provide a file containing an introspection query result - Provide a single SDL file containing your schema - Provide an array of multiple SDL files to be merged into a final schema - Provide a glob that leads to SDL files to be merged into a final schema - Will automatically generate documentation for all Types, Fields, Queries, Mutations, Arguments and Subscriptions by default. - Supports blacklisting entire areas (e.g. "don't show Mutations") and 1-off blacklisting. - Supports providing examples via static metadata, or dynamically via a custom generator plugin that you control. - Supports (beta) dynamic grouping and arranging of data via a custom data arrangement plugin that you control. - Supports light tweaking or complete customization of CSS, JS and HTML output via themes. - Supports markdown just about everywhere you can provide text. - Live preview mode while developing. - Many options for output: - Specify a logo - Specify a favicon - Specify the target directory and HTML file name - Can output JS and/or CSS "in line" in your HTML file, rather than as separate files. - Can output in "embeddable" mode (only the `` content is generated) so output can be integrated into your existing site. - ...and more! ## Getting Started 1. Install SpectaQL: ```sh npm install -g spectaql # OR yarn global add spectaql ``` This is a global installation, but you can also either: - Clone this repository - Add `spectaql` as a dependency to an existing project. 2. Define a `config.yml` that specifies how you'd like to generate your docs. See [YAML Options](#yaml-options) for more. 3. Generate your docs! ```sh npx spectaql config.yml ``` Your generated documentation will be located in the `public` directory by default. You can either copy the generated HTML to your web server, write the output to somewhere else using the `-t /path/to/ouputDir` option, or add `-D` flag and view your docs live by pointing your browser to [http://localhost:4400/](http://localhost:4400/). ## Examples The best way to figure out what SpectaQL can do is to clone this repository (or mimic the [`/examples`](https://github.com/anvilco/spectaql/blob/main/examples) directory) and play around with the example build and its data: ```sh npm install npm run develop ./examples/config.yml ``` That config will direct a build that flexes the most interesting parts of SpectaQL, so dig in a little and it should be a rewarding exercise. ## YAML Options To generate your documentation, SpectaQL requires a configuration YAML. This file is where you can specify most of the options to make your output the way you'd like it. All the supported options and their descriptions can be found in the [`config-example.yml`](https://github.com/anvilco/spectaql/blob/main/config-example.yml) file. Environment variable substitution will be performed, so feel free to use environment variables in your config. You can also see a minimal-ish working example YAML in the [examples/config.yml](https://github.com/anvilco/spectaql/blob/main/examples/config.yml) file. ## Command Line Options Several options are supported via the CLI. Some are exclusive to the CLI, while others are also possible to specify in the YAML config. Options specified in the CLI take precedence over those that exist in the YAML config. All the supported options and their descriptions can be found in [/src/cli.js][cli]. ## Using SpectaQL as a Dependency In addition to using SpectaQL as a global (or local) binary executable, it can also be used by your Node application as a dependency. More documentation will be coming soon but here is a quick example to get you started: ```node import { run } from 'spectaql' ... const spectaqlOptions = { specFile: 'path/to/your/config.yml', resolveWithOutput: true, } const { html } = await run(spectaqlOptions) ... ``` ## Metadata In our experience, nearly all of the stuff we need for the content of the documentation comes from things supported in GraphQL and introspection queries...but not everything. To supplement some things that are missing, SpectaQL provides support for including "metadata" about your schema that can be used when generating the output. The following options are currently supported: - `example`: When provided for a Scalar, Field or Argument, this value will be used as an "example" for the Field or Argument. It can be any value supported in JSON. - `examples`: Same as `example`, but allows an Array of examples to be provided, from which one random one will be used during generation. - `undocumented`: A Boolean value that can be provided on a Type, Field, Argument, Query or Mutation indicating that this item is _**not**_ to be included in the resulting output. Useful for 1-off hiding of things where the default was to show them. - `documented`: Just like `undocumented`, except it _**will**_ include it in the resulting output. Useful for 1-off showing of things where the default was to hide them. SpectaQL supports 3 ways to include metadata to be used during processing: 1. Include your metadata in the introspection query (or introspection query results file). This requires manipulation of your introspection query results either on their way out from the server, or once in an output file. At Anvil, we use Apollo Server and leverage [this plugin we wrote](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@anvilco/apollo-server-plugin-introspection-metadata) to "weave" our metadata into the introspection query results. [This example output](https://github.com/anvilco/spectaql/blob/main/examples/data/introspection-with-metadata.json) illustrates what an "interwoven" metadata scenario might look like. 2. Provide a standalone JSON file containing your metadata to be "woven" into your introspection query results by SpectaQL. SpectaQL uses the `addMetadata` method from [our Apollo Plugin](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@anvilco/apollo-server-plugin-introspection-metadata) under the hood, so please see the documentation there or [this example](https://github.com/anvilco/spectaql/blob/main/examples/data/metadata.json) file to understand its format. 3. If you are providing your schema information via an SDL file or files, leverage the [SpectaQL Directive](#the-spectaql-directive) feature to provide your metadata in your SDL through directives. See the [SpectaQL Directive](#the-spectaql-directive) for more information. **NOTE**: Another way to ensure that things are not documented is to pass your GraphQL Introspection Results through [Microfiber][microfiber] and perform the removal of any Types, Fields, Queries etc before they even reach SpectaQL. Just a thought. ## The SpectaQL Directive If you are providing your schema information via an SDL file or files, you can leverage the `@spectaql` directive and pass any of the supported `metadata` options to the `options` argument of that directive. This is a useful approach for those who are taking an "SDL-first" development approach. See the `spectaqlDirective` option in the [`config-example.yml`](https://github.com/anvilco/spectaql/blob/main/config-example.yml) file for more information on how to enable and implement this approach. Here's what you need to know: - See the `spectaqlDirective` option in the [`config-example.yml`](https://github.com/anvilco/spectaql/blob/main/config-example.yml) file for information on how to enable and tweak this approach. - The directive's `options` argument is a List/Array of Input Objects that have the following shape: ``` { key: String!, value: String! } ``` - All the `value` fields should be provided as strings, and they will be appropriately parsed based on the supported value of the `key` field. - SpectaQL does not need you to add the definition of the `spectaql` directive, nor its `SpectaQLOption` input type to your SDL. They will be added (and removed) by SpectaQL automatically if you enable the feature. However, if you are using that same SDL to create an executable schema, you will need to add the directive and options definitions for your server to not complain. - The directive can be added to your SDL anywhere that directives are supported by GraphQL SDL syntax, but they may only have an impact on the areas that SpectaQL supports. The directive-related SDL is: ```sdl directive @spectaql(options: [SpectaQLOption]) on QUERY | MUTATION | SUBSCRIPTION | FIELD | FRAGMENT_DEFINITION | FRAGMENT_SPREAD | INLINE_FRAGMENT | VARIABLE_DEFINITION | SCHEMA | SCALAR | OBJECT | FIELD_DEFINITION | ARGUMENT_DEFINITION | INTERFACE | UNION | ENUM | ENUM_VALUE | INPUT_OBJECT | INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION input SpectaQLOption { key: String!, value: String! } ```` Or you can generate the required directive SDL programmatically like so: ```node import { generateSpectaqlSdl } from 'spectaql' const spectaqlSdl = generateSpectaqlSdl() // Do something with this SDL ``` Once enabled, the directive can be used like so: ```sdl type MyType {

scout.summary: Hulunote is an open-source, AI-enhanced outliner note-taking app inspired by Roam Research, emphasizing bidirectional linking and knowledge organization. It integrates deeply with the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to enable AI agents to perform reflective reasoning, batch operations, and autonomous note management. The app supports multiple databases, daily notes, and a built-in MCP client, positioning itself as a 'note hippocampus' powered by AI.
highlights: Built-in MCP client enables AI-driven note operations and integration with external AI tools like Claude Desktop.
Supports MCP reflective reasoning for multi-step, structured AI interactions with notes (v1.1.0).
Offers autonomous note management via OpenClaw agent platform integration.
Core features include outliner structure, bidirectional links, daily notes, and multiple databases.
Architected with Clojure/ClojureScript and Rust (Axum, SQLx), indicating a hybrid frontend-backend design.
risks: Heavy reliance on emerging MCP ecosystem may limit interoperability or adoption if standards shift.
Limited community size (74 stars, 12 forks) could impact long-term maintenance and feature velocity.
Only two releases to date; rapid feature addition (e.g., MCP reasoning in v1.1.0) may introduce stability risks.

inventor.summary: Hulunote innovates by merging outliner-based knowledge management with AI-driven reasoning via the Model Context Protocol (MCP), enabling reflective, multi-step AI operations on structured notes. It functions as a programmable 'note hippocampus' where AI agents can autonomously organize, generate, and restructure knowledge.
highlights: Introduces MCP reflective reasoning to note-taking, allowing AI to analyze structure and execute batch operations with multi-step planning [ID:E-RL-001].
Embeds a native MCP client, transforming the app into an AI tool hub compatible with external agents like Claude Desktop [ID:E-RD-003][ID:E-RL-002].
Enables natural language control of note databases via hulunote-mcp-server, supporting commands like 'Create a note about quantum computing' [ID:E-RD-005][ID:E-RD-006].
Extends autonomy through OpenClaw integration, allowing AI agents to manage notes without manual input [ID:E-RD-007][ID:E-RD-008].
Combines hierarchical outlining with bidirectional linking and daily notes, enhanced by AI co-pilot capabilities under a unified 'AI + Note Hippocampus' vision [ID:E-RD-001][ID:E-RD-004].
risks: Tight coupling to the nascent MCP standard may hinder adoption if the protocol evolves or fails to gain traction [ID:E-RL-001][ID:E-RL-002].
Early-stage maturity (only two releases) combined with advanced AI features could lead to reliability or usability issues in complex workflows [ID:E-RL-001][ID:E-RL-002].
Dependency on external AI platforms (e.g., OpenClaw, Claude) introduces integration fragility and potential vendor lock-in [ID:E-RD-007][ID:E-RD-005].

engineer.summary: Hulunote is a Clojure/ClojureScript-based outliner note-taking application with deep integration of the Model Context Protocol (MCP), enabling AI agents to perform reflective reasoning, batch operations, and autonomous knowledge management. It features a hybrid architecture using Rust (Axum, SQLx) for backend services and Datascript for local data modeling, supporting bidirectional linking, daily notes, and multiple databases.
highlights: Built-in MCP client allows direct integration with AI tools like Claude Desktop and custom MCP servers for programmatic note operations [ID:E-RD-003][ID:E-RL-002].
MCP reflective reasoning (v1.1.0) enables multi-step AI analysis and structured batch actions on note hierarchies [ID:E-RL-001].
Hybrid tech stack: frontend in ClojureScript (with Shadow CLJS), backend services in Rust, and Datascript for client-side graph storage [ID:E-RF-001][ID:E-RL-002].
Extensible via hulunote-mcp-server and OpenClaw agent platform, supporting natural language commands and autonomous plugins [ID:E-RD-005][ID:E-RD-007][ID:E-RD-008].
Core note-taking features—outliner structure, bidirectional links, daily notes, and multi-database support—are enhanced by AI co-pilot functionality [ID:E-RD-001][ID:E-RD-003].
risks: Tight coupling to the emerging MCP standard may create compatibility risks if the protocol changes or lacks broad adoption [ID:E-RL-001][ID:E-RL-002].
Limited release history (only v0.1.0 and v1.1.0) suggests potential instability, especially with advanced AI features layered atop a complex Clojure/Rust stack [ID:E-RL-001][ID:E-RL-002].
Integration dependencies on external platforms (OpenClaw, Claude) could introduce maintenance overhead or breakage if those ecosystems evolve independently [ID:E-RD-007][ID:E-RD-005].

skeptic.summary: Hulunote presents an ambitious vision of AI-augmented note-taking via MCP integration, but its claims of advanced AI reasoning and autonomous operation lack substantiating implementation details or community validation, raising concerns about feasibility, overpromising, and dependency on unstable external ecosystems.
highlights: Claims of 'MCP reflective reasoning' enabling AI to 'think before acting' [ID:E-RL-001] are vague and unsupported by technical documentation or evidence of actual reasoning fidelity.

The app positions itself as a local AI tool hub [ID:E-RL-002], yet relies entirely on external, potentially volatile integrations (Claude Desktop, OpenClaw) with no fallback or offline AI capability described.
Despite touting 'autonomous note management' via OpenClaw [ID:E-RD-007], the project provides no examples, tests, or safeguards for AI-driven modifications to user data.
The 'AI + Note Hippocampus' metaphor [ID:E-RD-004] is evocative but masks a lack of concrete evidence that AI features meaningfully enhance—rather than complicate—core note-taking workflows.
risks: Overreliance on unproven MCP ecosystem: if MCP adoption stalls or changes, core advertised functionality becomes obsolete [ID:E-RL-001][ID:E-RL-002].
No visible error handling, user control, or audit trails for AI-driven batch operations, risking unintended data corruption [ID:E-RL-001].
Extremely limited release history (only two versions) combined with grandiose AI claims suggests vaporware risk—features may be aspirational rather than functional [ID:E-RL-001][ID:E-RL-002].
Zero open issues [repo_profile] may indicate lack of real-world usage or testing, not stability—raising doubts about robustness of AI integrations.

scout.summary: WPPConnect is an active open-source TypeScript library that bridges WhatsApp Web functionality to Node.js, enabling automation for chatbots, customer service, and media interactions. It shows consistent maintenance with recent releases, community engagement via Discord/Telegram, and awareness of WhatsApp's evolving interface.
highlights: Exports WhatsApp Web features to Node.js for building bots, AI integrations, and messaging workflows [ID:E-RD-002].
Actively maintained with recent patch and feature releases addressing bugs and updating dependencies like @wppconnect/wa-version [ID:E-RL-001, E-RL-002, E-RL-003].
Rich feature set including multi-session support, media/message handling, contact/group management, and location sharing [ID:E-RD-005].
Strong community presence with Discord, Telegram, and WhatsApp groups, plus external documentation at wppconnect.io [ID:E-RD-004, E-RD-003, E-RD-006].
risks: Reliance on WhatsApp Web internals makes the project vulnerable to breaking changes; maintainers explicitly advise keeping the package updated [ID:E-RD-008].
Open dependency dashboard issue suggests potential technical debt or pending updates [ID:E-IS-001].

inventor.summary: WPPConnect enables novel automation and integration scenarios by exposing WhatsApp Web's capabilities through a Node.js API, supporting use cases like AI-driven customer service bots, multi-session messaging systems, and rich media workflows.
highlights: Provides programmatic access to WhatsApp Web features including sending text/media, managing contacts/groups, and handling multiple sessions—enabling inventive chatbot and automation solutions [ID:E-RD-005].
Supports AI and intelligence-based interactions via phrase recognition and message processing, aligning with modern conversational AI development [ID:E-RD-002].
Offers extensible architecture with documented APIs and event streams, facilitating custom integrations and real-time message handling [ID:E-RD-006, E-RL-003].
Actively evolves with WhatsApp Web through frequent dependency updates (e.g., @wppconnect/wa-version), ensuring continued compatibility for inventive applications [ID:E-RL-001, E-RL-003].
risks: Innovation is constrained by WhatsApp Web’s undocumented and shifting internals; any interface change could break core functionality despite update efforts [ID:E-RD-008].
Dependency management overhead is evident from open dashboard issues, potentially delaying adoption of new WhatsApp features or security patches [ID:E-IS-001].

engineer.summary: WPPConnect is a TypeScript-based Node.js library that reverse-engineers WhatsApp Web to provide a programmable API for messaging automation, with active maintenance, comprehensive feature coverage, and awareness of compatibility risks.
highlights: Built in TypeScript with a structured project layout including src, tests, linting (eslint, prettier), and CI workflows (.github, husky) [ID:E-RF-001].
Exports a wide range of WhatsApp Web capabilities: multi-session support, media/contact/group management, location sharing, and message forwarding [ID:E-RD-005].

Actively maintained with recent releases fixing bugs (e.g., 't is not a function') and updating critical dependencies like @wppconnect/wa-version to track WhatsApp Web changes [ID:E-RL-002, E-RL-001, E-RL-003].
Well-documented with external tutorials, API references, and getting-started guides hosted at wppconnect.io [ID:E-RD-003, E-RD-006, E-RD-007].
risks: High coupling to WhatsApp Web’s internal implementation; maintainers explicitly warn users to stay updated due to frequent breaking changes [ID:E-RD-008].
Open dependency dashboard issue indicates potential technical debt or lagging updates in the dependency chain [ID:E-IS-001].

skeptic.summary: While WPPConnect presents an ambitious interface to WhatsApp Web, its viability is undermined by inherent instability due to reverse-engineering, opaque maintenance practices, and unresolved dependency issues.
highlights: The project openly admits reliance on WhatsApp Web's volatile internals, requiring constant updates just to maintain basic functionality [ID:E-RD-008].
Despite claims of active maintenance, a standing 'Dependency Dashboard' issue suggests unaddressed technical debt or neglected updates [ID:E-IS-001].
Recent releases focus narrowly on dependency bumps and cryptic bug fixes (e.g., 't is not a function'), offering little transparency into stability or regression testing [ID:E-RL-002, E-RL-001].
Feature completeness claims (e.g., AI integration, multi-session support) lack evidence of robustness or real-world validation in the provided materials [ID:E-RD-002, E-RD-005].
risks: Reverse-engineering WhatsApp Web violates WhatsApp’s Terms of Service, exposing users to potential account bans or legal risk—unacknowledged in documentation [ID:E-RD-002].
No clear strategy for handling breaking changes beyond 'update frequently,' leaving production systems vulnerable to sudden failure [ID:E-RD-008].
Community support channels (Discord, Telegram) may mask poor code quality or inadequate error handling, as seen in vague bug fixes [ID:E-RD-004, E-RL-002].

scout.summary: Altimate Code is an open-source agentic data engineering CLI tool that provides AI-powered SQL analysis, column-level lineage, and integrations with dbt and major cloud warehouses. It supports terminal, LLM-embedded, and CI/CD usage, with recent releases focusing on TUI improvements, telemetry, and distribution channels. However, several unresolved bugs and packaging issues may impact usability.
highlights: Offers 100+ deterministic tools for SQL, dbt, FinOps, and multi-cloud warehouse connectivity.
Supports multiple deployment modes: standalone TUI, LLM integration (Claude/Codex), and CI pipelines.
Auto-discovers data stack components like dbt projects and warehouse credentials from common sources.
Actively maintained with recent releases (v0.5.0–v0.5.2) adding features like streaming TUI, built-in skills, and improved logging control.
Distributed via npm and previously Homebrew; exploring Docker and curl-pipe installers.
risks: Open bug reports indicate instability in warehouse connection configuration and native dependency issues (e.g., GLIBC_2.35, better-sqlite3 with Bun).
Critical components like dbt-tools and setup utilities were missing from shipped npm binaries (now closed, but recurrence risk remains).
Homebrew installation was removed from docs and README, potentially confusing users expecting multi-channel support.
Reliance on environment variables or interactive TUI for LLM API key setup may complicate headless or automated deployments.

inventor.summary: Altimate Code is an agentic data engineering platform that combines deterministic tooling with LLM integration to automate SQL analysis, lineage tracking, and cloud warehouse operations. It introduces a novel 'harness' architecture enabling AI agents to safely interact with production data stacks via CLI, TUI, or embedded modes, while emphasizing read-only discovery and fine-grained access control.
highlights: Pioneers an 'agentic data engineering harness' model that bridges LLMs (Claude, Codex) with 100+ deterministic tools for SQL, dbt, and FinOps across 10 cloud warehouses.
Introduces a terminal-based TUI with streaming responses, trace history, and built-in commands like /discover and /connect for zero-config data stack onboarding.
Implements safe, read-only auto-discovery of dbt projects and warehouse credentials from ~/.dbt/profiles.yml, Docker, and env vars—enabling agent context without manual setup.
Architects modular distribution via npm with plans for Homebrew, Docker, and curl-pipe, though recent removal of Brew from docs suggests channel instability.
Enforces SQL write access control and PII-safe telemetry, indicating deliberate design for production-safe AI agent deployment.
risks: Native dependency fragility: better-sqlite3 breaks Bun builds and GLIBC_2.35 issues suggest poor cross-platform binary compatibility.
Incomplete packaging: critical components (dbt-tools, altimate-setup) were omitted from npm bundles, risking broken installs despite being marked fixed.
Unreliable multi-channel distribution: Homebrew support was removed from docs mid-stream, undermining user trust in alternative install paths.
LLM key management relies on interactive TUI or env vars, complicating secure, automated CI/CD or headless deployments.

engineer.summary: Altimate Code is a TypeScript-based CLI/TUI tool that provides AI-assisted data engineering capabilities through deterministic SQL, dbt, and warehouse integrations. It supports multiple deployment modes and LLM backends but faces packaging, dependency, and distribution challenges that impact reliability and cross-platform compatibility.
highlights: Built with TypeScript and uses Bun (evidenced by bun.lock and bunfig.toml), with modular monorepo structure (packages/, turbo.json).
Offers terminal-based TUI with streaming responses, trace history, and built-in commands like /discover and /connect for seamless data stack integration.
Auto-discovers dbt projects and warehouse credentials from standard locations (~/.dbt/profiles.yml, env vars, Docker) in a read-only, production-safe manner.
Actively developed with frequent releases (v0.5.0–v0.5.2) addressing TUI stability, logging suppression, telemetry, and SQL injection hardening.
Distributed primarily via npm; Homebrew support was recently removed from docs despite being listed in install instructions.
risks: Native dependency issues: better-sqlite3 breaks Bun builds (E-IS-004) and GLIBC_2.35 errors (E-IS-005) indicate poor Linux binary portability.
Packaging gaps: critical components (dbt-tools, altimate-setup) were missing from npm bundles (E-IS-002), risking non-functional installs.
Inconsistent distribution strategy: Homebrew installation referenced in releases (E-RL-001, E-RL-002, E-RL-003) but removed from docs (E-RL-001), causing user confusion.
Open bugs in core functionality: warehouse connection misconfiguration (E-IS-001) remains unresolved, potentially blocking cloud integrations.

skeptic.summary: Altimate Code markets itself as a robust, production-ready agentic data engineering platform, but evidence reveals significant instability in core functionality, packaging, and cross-platform support—raising doubts about its readiness for real-world use.
highlights: Claims '100+ deterministic tools' and 'precision data tooling', yet critical components like dbt-tools were missing from shipped npm binaries (now closed, but indicative of QA gaps).

Promotes multi-channel distribution (npm, Homebrew, Docker), but Homebrew was quietly removed from docs despite still appearing in release install instructions—suggesting misleading or inconsistent messaging.
Advertises seamless warehouse connectivity, yet an open bug (E-IS-001) confirms incorrect warehouse connection configuration remains unresolved.
Touts compatibility with modern runtimes like Bun, but schema indexing fails due to native dependency on better-sqlite3 (E-IS-004), exposing poor runtime portability.
risks: Users may encounter broken installations due to past npm packaging failures and ongoing native binary issues (GLIBC_2.35, better-sqlite3), especially on non-standard or older Linux systems.
Reliance on interactive TUI or environment variables for LLM API keys complicates secure automation, contradicting claims of CI/CD integration readiness.
Removal of Homebrew from documentation while retaining it in release notes creates confusion and erodes trust in installation reliability.
Open bugs in foundational features (warehouse connections, runtime compatibility) suggest the project prioritizes feature velocity over stability.

scout.summary: SpectaQL is a well-maintained Node.js library for generating static GraphQL API documentation with flexible input options and strong customization support. It is actively used and developed, but has some unresolved security concerns.
highlights: Generates static GraphQL documentation from live endpoints, introspection results, or SDL files.
Offers modern, customizable 3-column layout out of the box.
Actively maintained with clear changelogs and breaking change notes.
Part of Anvil’s suite of developer-focused PDF and e-signature APIs.
risks: Open high-severity npm audit issues reported in version 3.0.8.
Past vulnerability related to Lodash.unset (now closed, but indicates dependency risk).

inventor.summary: SpectaQL is an inventive solution that automates GraphQL API documentation generation with flexible input sources and a modern, customizable UI, reducing manual upkeep while integrating seamlessly into developer workflows.
highlights: Innovatively supports three distinct schema input methods: live introspection, saved introspection results, and SDL files, offering broad compatibility.
Delivers a polished, responsive 3-column documentation layout by default, balancing aesthetics and usability without configuration.
Enables deep customization for advanced users while remaining accessible out-of-the-box, lowering the barrier to high-quality docs.
Tightly aligned with Anvil’s ecosystem of document automation APIs, suggesting intentional design for real-world developer pain points.
risks: Security vulnerabilities in dependencies (e.g., Lodash.unset CVE) indicate potential risks in third-party code reuse without sufficient vetting.
Open high-severity npm audit issues may deter adoption in security-conscious environments despite functional innovation.

engineer.summary: SpectaQL is a practical, well-structured Node.js tool for generating static GraphQL documentation with flexible input options and strong customization capabilities, though it carries notable dependency-related security risks that require attention.
highlights: Supports three schema ingestion methods: live introspection, saved introspection JSON, and SDL files—enabling integration into diverse CI/CD and dev workflows.
Delivers a modern, responsive 3-column UI by default with extensive theming and layout customization options.
Project structure includes standard engineering practices: ESLint, Prettier, Husky, lint-staged, and example configs, indicating mature development hygiene.
Clear documentation of breaking changes and changelog shows disciplined release management.
risks: Open high-severity npm audit issues in version 3.0.8 ([ID:E-IS-001]) pose potential security exposure in production deployments.
Historical Lodash.unset CVE ([ID:E-IS-002]) suggests dependency update lag, raising concerns about transitive vulnerability response time.
Runtime error reported in introspection example generation ([ID:E-IS-003]) indicates possible edge-case fragility in schema processing logic.

skeptic.summary: While SpectaQL offers convenient GraphQL documentation generation, its unresolved security flaws, dependency vulnerabilities, and runtime instability raise serious concerns about reliability and production readiness.
highlights: Despite claims of modern, maintainable documentation, the project has an open high-severity npm audit issue in its latest tagged version (3.0.8).
Past CVE in Lodash.unset suggests inadequate dependency hygiene, undermining trust in the project's security posture.
Reported runtime crash during introspection example generation indicates potential fragility in core functionality.
risks: Active high-severity security vulnerability ([ID:E-IS-001]) remains unpatched, posing a direct risk to users deploying SpectaQL in sensitive environments.
Dependency on vulnerable or outdated packages (e.g., Lodash.unset CVE, [ID:E-IS-002]) reflects poor supply chain management.
Core schema processing logic may fail unexpectedly on valid inputs, as shown by the 'Cannot read properties of undefined' error ([ID:E-IS-003]), reducing confidence in robustness.
